Oh, what's going on everyone Steve Larsen welcome to the one funnel way challenge day three day three guess I'm not in the padded room. I'm here in my home office And this is where I do all of my personal work and personal stuff I did not have time to go into the actual click phones office today, so I am not there right now obviously hey today Day three is all about the who not the how okay, and the first time I realized this I was mowing my lawn and I was mowing the lawn and I was listening to I think I was listening to the Russell and several others and I realized that that I was trying to learn too many things in my business okay The first time I actually learned about this though. I was this is gonna sound really weird I was playing dodgeball with tennis balls and anytime you start a sentence that way You know it's gonna be a great story okay, but every single week what we would do is we would go There's about 40 or 50 of us who would go and we would play dodgeball with tennis balls Okay, and it was fun because it hurt and we would go in this gym And we'd you know get all these tables down as barriers and just dump a ton of tennis balls there And we played dodgeball tennis balls, and it was a lot of fun, and I remember we used to do that every week and We would go in We used to go into this room right and we We'd split into teams and we'd go and get started and what was interesting what was what was cool. Can you see me? Okay, I hope you guys can hear me What was cool is I? Thank you very much appreciate that This one time I walk on in and no one was getting started and this was a super fun tradition We were having so I was like I want to go let's do this would be super fun And what I did is I went and I was like hey, let's get started. Let's get started I started telling everybody let's go and get started nobody moved There was no one that moved for about 10 minutes, and I got so frustrated. I started I started leaving Okay, I and I was like this is this is what are we doing here? and as I'm leaving I run into this guy on the way out and He goes what's wrong? I said dude. I'm trying to get everybody to get going no one will get going and I kid you not This is a weird lesson, but I promise this is how I learned it and you guys will remember it now, too Okay, this is how I learned this I kid you not he lifted his head He's not even around everybody who lives in his head and he goes hey everybody We should get started in this big gym with 40 people and Everybody stopped and turned and looked and we divided in teams we started And that didn't leave me and there was actually a notebook that I used to carry with me at all times I can't find right now, but it's a black notebook is about this big and I would write down all of the business lessons I was experiencing in life and what I wrote down that day as we were playing dodgeball with tennis balls was oh my gosh I don't have to be the trombonist in the violinist and I don't have to be the the I don't have to be this person and that person and this person and that person what was crazy I have to be the orchestrator. That's it all Entrepreneurship is about being an orchestrator. It's not about knowing how to play every instrument game in the symphony it's not and Which sucks cave because a lot of people like me if you like me at all the like man I Want to go how do I do this how do I write copy how to do video how to do sound editing? See when I see all your film stuff in the back like you know what I mean I gotta do that that that that that that that that that that that and then like it is a long road It's a super long road when you do that way and I took me while to just follow my own advice even that was 10 years ago when that happened and And I always kept that in my head and what was interesting is as I started working for click funnels And as I started seeing what these other entrepreneurs were doing the thing that they had that I did not Because I had been studying the books right how many guys have read expert secrets how many guys read dotcom secrets Right how many guys have been going through each of those what I what was interesting is is as I started watching each one of these people The thing they had that I did not was a team That was it In fact, and I'm not I'm not going oh look at me But like in a lot of cases I might have known more than them right I might have had more experience with this area more experience with this area the thing that I sucked it up at Was having a team I was trying to be a renaissance man kill the renaissance man people do not be Every role in your business. I got all these I've got all these things I need to get done And I'm trying to learn the house I gotta stop that I gotta cut that out. I gotta go find the right hoos and so what I did is I started getting on different sites um hiring virtual assistants and hiring vAs and little odd job projects and I didn't have a lot of money So I went and I did a little bit of I did a small project got a couple hundred dollars And then I went and I started on like freelancer.com or you can use like funnel rolladex.com right and you go hire out The specific task and roll that you're needing pay them the money And then when I was at my nine to five job with russell crap was getting done Okay, that's how I did it and so I would wake up at 5 a.m And I would ride my bike into click funnel's office because I was still kind of poor Right and we didn't have another car and I would ride my bike from and I would be in the office at 6 a.m And from 6 a.m to 9 a.m for a year and a half I would build my business and when russell walked in at 9 a.m I would switch gears and I would build his business. Okay, and then I would go back home and uh in the evening time I play you know play with my kids we'd hang out with the fam and then I went in and uh it was like from nine P.m. It's usually midnight. I'd work again and I'd be talking to more whose That's really what I was doing. I was I was making sure the team the team even though it was virtual What had the things they needed for me and then I'd sleep five hours get up and I'd do it again And that's how I built it. I don't know how to do this Right. And so what this is what we did guys. This is to illustrate the point. This is to illustrate the point Okay, this is what I did I we went in and we started walking around to people on campus and we started saying hey You know, if there was like a food you could buy on campus like what would it be? And we pulled and we surveyed and we pulled and we surveyed and we spent a day and we went back and the next day We started looking at all the things that they had said. Oh, you know what? Okay, that means they're gonna be wearing typically a few several different hats A funnel is a revenue stream A business is the systems that support the funnel That's it. That's all a business is And if you have no systems, you don't have a business If you treat every support ticket different, some of you guys like I don't even have a support system. That's fine. That's fine I'm just telling you you or the business. All right. Here we go. I have a system Okay, um, who who is the Who is solely responsible for revenue in the company? Yeah, you right the the actual entrepreneur is right understand that an entrepreneur Really is the one solely responsible for the funnel But an entrepreneur is not necessarily always a business owner Would you consider a mcdonald's franchise owner an entrepreneur? Not really. What are they? Entrepreneur, what are they creating nothing? They're operating a business They're not creating the business and the revenue. Does that make sense? You have to understand I am only focused on revenue generating activities, especially at the beginning Especially in the phase you are in It is all about understanding that we are we are acting as entrepreneurs not business owners Okay, they are not the same Okay So what this is I have a I have a thing that I call red dot green dot. Okay as we move forward in this As we move forward in this what I want you to start doing is I keep a piece of paper around me Or you can use something like trello if you never heard of that a lot of times I've got five whiteboards throughout my office actually All over the place and and I have whiteboards and sometimes they're just running Lists of tasks Okay, so this is what I would do Write down all of the tasks that you have to get done. Just brain dump. Don't hold them in your head You only have so much mental shelf space. Okay, it's almost like a ram for a computer. You know what I mean? You're you're operating space goes higher and higher the more tasks you held you held or you hold inside there So I write them down. I write down all the tasks that I have to get done I'm like, okay. Here's where I'm going I'm going to that peak. I can't see all the pieces in between But I can see enough to take the first step and maybe the second What do I need to do to take that first step and I'll brain dump? Well All right, I got to think about the funnel. All right. I got to think about the product Uh, I got to design those I got to write the copy. I got to write and there's there's several things that are needed to get a funnel out the door write them down What I do though is I don't just leave them there if I leave them there It is what creates noise in my head and I hate that if you guys can't tell I'm already kind of a loud dude Okay, I don't want I don't need more noise in my life. Okay already generate enough right here Okay, so I want a lot of noise in my head the way to do it is is for me to just make a massive to-do list Okay, don't Like don't stop there. First of all, I brain dump to get it out of my head second thing I do though is this right here every one of us has a portion of our day where our brains are more fresh Okay, my brain works most actively Right and and the best on hard problems from usually about 7 a.m. To about 1 p.m. It might be different for you. That's awesome That's great. Whatever just know what it is I work on the biggest revenue generating tasks during that time of my day Okay, and when I was working for somebody else was working for Russell or I was in college or whatever I would do those tasks in the morning Okay, I would do smaller revenue generating activities in the evening So what I do is I have this big massive list of all the stuff I feel like I need to get done in order to make this project out go out the door I go back through the list and I'm ruthless guys I take a green marker and I put a big massive green dot next to the hardest Revenue generating activities that I have to complete things like writing copy coming up with an offer Right building the funnel. Those are mentally tasking right or mentally taxing activities So I put a big green dot next to him I'm only going to do those during the hardest right only the freshest part of the brain parts of the day So you only have so much decision-making power. Okay Then what I go back to it through is I put a small green dot next to all the ones that are smaller But still revenue generating right things like creating a relationship with this person that might say my name and bring in some sales Right, that's not a big task that I have to really do a lot of work for Okay, I'll go back through so I call it red dot green dot So I go back and I read green dot all of the big massive Revenue ones small green dot all the ones that are a little bit Uh, um, there's still revenue generating but just not like as hard to complete right versus like build a funnel You know what I mean? That's like it's mentally taxing. Um I put a red dot next to all the ones that are not revenue generating But still need to get done at some point I never do a red dot Okay Things like make a logo. I didn't have a logo for the first seven months Why because it doesn't make revenue and it's a fake thing that is not as important as we all wish it was Okay You know what I'm saying go back through and don't do red dots. Okay, don't do red dots this entire challenge Don't do any of them. Don't do any of them. You only focus on revenue generating activities and your other Who's are the ones who do the red dots in the future? I don't have to do red dots. I don't handle support because it doesn't generate revenue Right. I don't handle shipping up boxes and sending it out because it doesn't it doesn't have anything to do with revenue Okay, I only do revenue generating big green dot activities and then sometimes like in the evening If I've got an extra 30 minutes before I go to bed or whatever, then I'll do like one or two green small dot activities This is huge Red dot green dot red dot green dot big revenue things versus small So when we go into the challenge understand that's what I want you to focus on There'll be very specific things that we're bringing you through But it's because we're taking you somewhere and we have already pre-thought a lot of things for you where you're like Don't even worry about the garbage right there. Okay. Don't even worry about it right there Don't even worry about it right there. Just look big green dot big green dot big green dot massive revenue massive revenue massive revenue Yeah Yeah, okay that will clear up a lot of the noise in the head It will also relieve you from a lot of the stress and those of you are perfectionists like I am It will help you not be perfectionist, right? It will help you get all your crap done to a good good enough level to sell Not a it has to be perfect level because you'll never launch if it's perfect Um, because it's it just doesn't work that way.
